strengths: Currently building production agentic AI infrastructure at re:cinq — MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — giving him direct, hands-on experience with the exact technical mandate this role centers on.

Proven distributed-systems depth at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with microservices, async I/O, and low-latency data pipelines — precisely the substrate needed for real-time fintech inference and event-streaming architectures.

Fintech-adjacent experience across RIDE Capital (investment platform, TDD-driven delivery, GraphQL APIs) and Optilyz (pricing optimization across multi-country configurations) demonstrates comfort operating in regulated, commercially consequential product contexts.
concerns: No documented engineering management or team-scaling experience; entire 17-year career is as an individual contributor (Staff/Senior), with no evidence of building or leading a team of 10–30+ engineers.

Core fintech infrastructure requirements — credit systems, working-capital allocation, real-time counterparty risk, regulatory compliance across jurisdictions — are not evidenced anywhere in the resume.

Agentic AI work is very recent (April 2025, approximately three months) and scoped at staff IC level; no evidence of architecting or owning multi-agent systems at production scale with financial consequences.
response.justification: Bogdan brings rare, current, production-level experience building the agentic AI infrastructure — MCP frameworks, agent orchestration tooling — that sits at the heart of this role's technical mandate, combined with a proven foundation in high-throughput distributed systems from Dunnhumby and fintech-adjacent delivery at RIDE Capital and Optilyz; a board willing to invest in a technically exceptional CTO who will grow into the executive leadership and team-scaling dimensions of the role, rather than hiring a polished manager with shallower AI depth, could find in Bogdan a high-upside principal whose hands-on agentic systems expertise is genuinely uncommon at any seniority level.
